structured_payload_candidate.title: Relief Damper Modulation for Direct Building Pressure Control

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Status Mismatch Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Status differs from command for 15 seconds
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm if commanded ON but status OFF; Level 4 if commanded OFF but status ON
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2 b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4

evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2 b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4
evidence_text: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2 b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule: fan status mismatch alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#2
structured_payload_candidate.summary: FC#2: MAT too low; should be between OAT and RAT. Equation: MAT_avg + ε_MAT < min[(RAT_avg - ε_RAT), (OAT_avg - ε_OAT)]. Applies to OS#1–#5.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault with possible diagnoses: RAT, MAT, or OAT sensor error
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #2 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: MAT AVG + Ɛ MAT < min[(RAT AVG - ƐRAT), (OAT AVG - ƐOAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description: MAT too low; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis: RAT sensor error, MAT sensor error, OAT sensor error

evidence_quote: FC #2 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: MAT AVG + Ɛ MAT < min[(RAT AVG - ƐRAT), (OAT AVG - ƐOAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description: MAT too low; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis: RAT sensor error, MAT sensor error, OAT sensor error
evidence_text: FC #2 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: MAT AVG + Ɛ MAT < min[(RAT AVG - ƐRAT), (OAT AVG - ƐOAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description: MAT too low; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis: RAT sensor error, MAT sensor error, OAT sensor error
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule: AFDD FC#2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#10
structured_payload_candidate.summary: FC#10: OAT and MAT should be approximately equal. Equation: |MAT_avg - OAT_avg| > sqrt(ε_MAT^2 + ε_OAT^2). Applies to OS#3.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault with possible diagnoses: MAT or OAT sensor error, leaking economizer damper
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #10 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: | MAT AVG - OAT AVG | > √ƐMAT2+ ƐOAT2 Applies to OS #3 Description: OAT and MAT should be approximately equal Possible Diagnosis: MAT sensor error, OAT sensor error, Leaking or stuck economizer damper or actuator

evidence_quote: FC #10 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: | MAT AVG - OAT AVG | > √ƐMAT2+ ƐOAT2 Applies to OS #3 Description: OAT and MAT should be approximately equal Possible Diagnosis: MAT sensor error, OAT sensor error, Leaking or stuck economizer damper or actuator
evidence_text: FC #10 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: | MAT AVG - OAT AVG | > √ƐMAT2+ ƐOAT2 Applies to OS #3 Description: OAT and MAT should be approximately equal Possible Diagnosis: MAT sensor error, OAT sensor error, Leaking or stuck economizer damper or actuator
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule: AFDD FC#10.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Alarm Delay and Reporting
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition that evaluates as TRUE must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.

evidence_text: A fault condition that evaluates as TRUE must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Test Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 for TestModeDelay minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.

evidence_quote: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.
evidence_text: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Evaluation Suspension Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended when AHU is not operating and for a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode of any Zone Group served by the AHU.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: AHU not operating or mode change
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suspend fault condition evaluation
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: a. When AHU is not operating b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the AHU

evidence_quote: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: a. When AHU is not operating b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the AHU
evidence_text: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: a. When AHU is not operating b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the AHU
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule: AFDD evaluation suspension conditions.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Medium Fan Speed Reset Based on OAT
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Medium fan speed MedSpeed is reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature from MinSpeed when OAT ≥ Endpoint #1 to MaxCoolSpeed when OAT ≤ Endpoint #2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Air Temperature Deadband Setpoint

structured_payload_candidate.summary: The Deadband values of SATsp and SATsp-C shall be the average of the zone heating setpoint and the zone cooling setpoint but shall be no lower than 21°C (70°F) and no higher than 24°C (75°F).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set SATsp and SATsp-C to the average of zone heating and cooling setpoints, clamped between 21°C and 24°C
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Deadband values of SATsp and SATsp-C shall be the average of the zone heating setpoint and the zone cooling setpoint but shall be no lower than 21°C (70°F) and no higher than 24°C (75°F).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Speed and SAT Setpoint Control Map
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When supply fan is proven ON, fan speed and SAT setpoints are controlled as per Figures 5.18.4.5-1 through 5.18.4.5-3, with separate gains for each section of the control map.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Control fan speed and SAT setpoints according to the control diagram; provide separate gains for each section or adjust x-axis thresholds for stable control
configurable_values: Gains for each section of control map
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the supply fan is proven ON, fan speed and supply air temperature setpoints are controlled as shown in Figures 5.18.4.5-1 through 5.18.4.5-3. The points of transition along the x-axis shown and described are representative. Separate gains shall be provided for each section of the control map, that are determined by the contractor to provide stable control. Alternatively, the contractor shall adjust the precise value of the x-axis thresholds shown in Figure 5.18.4.5-1 to provide stable control.

evidence_quote: When the supply fan is proven ON, fan speed and supply air temperature setpoints are controlled as shown in Figures 5.18.4.5-1 through 5.18.4.5-3. The points of transition along the x-axis shown and described are representative. Separate gains shall be provided for each section of the control map, that are determined by the contractor to provide stable control. Alternatively, the contractor shall adjust the precise value of the x-axis thresholds shown in Figure 5.18.4.5-1 to provide stable control.
evidence_text: When the supply fan is proven ON, fan speed and supply air temperature setpoints are controlled as shown in Figures 5.18.4.5-1 through 5.18.4.5-3. The points of transition along the x-axis shown and described are representative. Separate gains shall be provided for each section of the control map, that are determined by the contractor to provide stable control. Alternatively, the contractor shall adjust the precise value of the x-axis thresholds shown in Figure 5.18.4.5-1 to provide stable control.
judge_reason: Operational sequence: fan speed and SAT control map.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Speed Reset in Heating and Cooling
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan speed is reset based on heating or cooling loop signal: from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed for 100% to 50% heating, MinSpeed for 50% to 0% heating and deadband, MinSpeed for 0% to 25% cooling, reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed for 25% to 50% cooling, MedSpeed for 50% to 75% cooling, and reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed for 75% to 100% cooling.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Heating or cooling loop signal value
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset fan speed according to the specified ranges
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed. b. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed. c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed. d. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed. e. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed. f. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed. g. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.

evidence_quote: a. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed. b. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed. c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed. d. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed. e. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed. f. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed. g. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.
evidence_text: a. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed. b. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed. c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed. d. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed. e. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed. f. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed. g. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.
judge_reason: Operational sequence: fan speed reset in heating and cooling.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Freeze Protection Stage 1
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two or more heating hot-water plant requests, override OA damper to minimum position, and modulate heating coil to maintain SAT of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable when SAT rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send heating plant requests, set OA damper to minimum, modulate heating coil to maintain SAT ≥ 6°C; disable when SAT > 7°C for 5 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.

evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
evidence_text: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
judge_reason: Operational sequence: freeze protection stage 1.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Operating State Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The OS of each AHU shall be defined by the commanded positions of heating coil valve, cooling coil valve, and economizer damper per Table 5.18.13.2.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Determine OS based on valve and damper positions
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The OS of each AHU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve, cooling-coil control valve, and economizer damper in accordance with Table 5.18.13.2 and Figure 5.18.13.2.

evidence_quote: The OS of each AHU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve, cooling-coil control valve, and economizer damper in accordance with Table 5.18.13.2 and Figure 5.18.13.2.
evidence_text: The OS of each AHU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve, cooling-coil control valve, and economizer damper in accordance with Table 5.18.13.2 and Figure 5.18.13.2.
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule: AFDD operating state definition.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#3
structured_payload_candidate.summary: FC#3: MAT too high; should be between OAT and RAT. Equation: MAT_avg - ε_MAT > min[(RAT_avg + ε_RAT), (OAT_avg + ε_OAT)]. Applies to OS#1–#5.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault with possible diagnoses: RAT, MAT, or OAT sensor error
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #3 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: MAT AVG - ƐMAT > min[(RAT AVG + Ɛ RAT), (OAT AVG + Ɛ OAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description: MAT too high; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis: RAT sensor error, MAT sensor error, OAT sensor error

evidence_quote: FC #3 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: MAT AVG - ƐMAT > min[(RAT AVG + Ɛ RAT), (OAT AVG + Ɛ OAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description: MAT too high; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis: RAT sensor error, MAT sensor error, OAT sensor error
evidence_text: FC #3 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: MAT AVG - ƐMAT > min[(RAT AVG + Ɛ RAT), (OAT AVG + Ɛ OAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description: MAT too high; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis: RAT sensor error, MAT sensor error, OAT sensor error
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule: AFDD FC#3.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#7
structured_payload_candidate.summary: FC#7: SAT too low in full heating. Equation: SAT_avg < SATsp - ε_SAT and HC ≥ 99%. Applies to OS#1.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault with possible diagnoses: sensor errors, valve issues, coil issues, etc.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #7 (omit if no heating coil) Equation: SAT AVG < SATSP - ƐSAT and HC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #1 Description: SAT too low in full heating Possible Diagnosis: SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve leaking or stuck open, Heating coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized heating coil, HW temperature too low or HW unavailable, Gas or electric heat is unavailable, DX cooling is stuck on, Leaking or stuck economizer damper or actuator

evidence_quote: FC #7 (omit if no heating coil) Equation: SAT AVG < SATSP - ƐSAT and HC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #1 Description: SAT too low in full heating Possible Diagnosis: SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve leaking or stuck open, Heating coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized heating coil, HW temperature too low or HW unavailable, Gas or electric heat is unavailable, DX cooling is stuck on, Leaking or stuck economizer damper or actuator
evidence_text: FC #7 (omit if no heating coil) Equation: SAT AVG < SATSP - ƐSAT and HC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #1 Description: SAT too low in full heating Possible Diagnosis: SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve leaking or stuck open, Heating coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized heating coil, HW temperature too low or HW unavailable, Gas or electric heat is unavailable, DX cooling is stuck on, Leaking or stuck economizer damper or actuator
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule: AFDD FC#7.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#13
structured_payload_candidate.summary: FC#13: SAT too high in full cooling. Equation: SAT_avg > SATsp-C + ε_SAT and CC ≥ 99%. Applies to OS#3, #4.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault with possible diagnoses: sensor errors, cooling coil issues, stuck heating, etc.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #13 Equation: SAT AVG > SAT SP-C + Ɛ SAT and CC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #3, #4 Description: SAT too high in full cooling Possible Diagnosis: SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized cooling coil, CHW temperature too low or CHW unavailable, DX cooling unavailable, Gas or electric heat stuck on, Heating coil valve leaking or stuck open

evidence_quote: FC #13 Equation: SAT AVG > SAT SP-C + Ɛ SAT and CC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #3, #4 Description: SAT too high in full cooling Possible Diagnosis: SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized cooling coil, CHW temperature too low or CHW unavailable, DX cooling unavailable, Gas or electric heat stuck on, Heating coil valve leaking or stuck open
evidence_text: FC #13 Equation: SAT AVG > SAT SP-C + Ɛ SAT and CC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #3, #4 Description: SAT too high in full cooling Possible Diagnosis: SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized cooling coil, CHW temperature too low or CHW unavailable, DX cooling unavailable, Gas or electric heat stuck on, Heating coil valve leaking or stuck open
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule: AFDD FC#13.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Alarm Level and Content
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When a fault condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the description of the fault and the list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.18.13.6.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 3 alarm with fault description and possible diagnoses
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When a fault condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the description of the fault and the list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.18.13.6.
